mnemonics: AGGREGATE FUNCTIONS: SUM, AVG, MIN, MAX, COUNT
keyPoints: Aggregate functions perform a calculation on a set of values and return a single value.
They are often used with the GROUP BY clause to group rows that have the same values into summary rows.
The HAVING clause is used to filter groups based on aggregate function results, unlike WHERE which filters individual rows.
commonMistakes: Using an aggregate function in a WHERE clause instead of a HAVING clause.
Forgetting to include all non-aggregated columns from the SELECT clause in the GROUP BY clause.
Confusing COUNT(*) which counts all rows with COUNT(column_name) which counts non-null values in a specific column.
